description: `SUBSCRIPTION` events reflect changes to a user's  subscription state. Most `SUBSCRIPTION` events represent changes to opt-in status. When the event represents a change to Subscription List memberships rather than a full opt-in or opt-out, the body includes a `subscription_lists` payload and no opt-out timestamps are set on the channel.

event_type.description:
Determines the source of the subscription event. `registration` and `create_and_send` events result in changes to `opted_in` dates. `link_unsubscribe` and `list_unsubscribe` events represent changes to `opted_out` status unless the body includes a `subscription_lists` payload, in which case the change applies only to the listed Subscription Lists. All other event types contain `opted_out` dates.

* `registration`: Represents a change to a user's `commercial_opted_in`, `transactional_opted_in`, `click_tracking_opted_in`, or `open_tracking_opted_in` dates.
* `create_and_send`: A user's opted_in status changed as a result of a [Create and Send](/guides/audience/segmentation/bulk-sending/) event.
* `bounce`: Subscription status changed as a result of a bounce event.
* `link_unsubscribe`: The user clicked an unsubscribe link in the message body of an email, or selected the **Unsubscribe from all** button in the Email Preference Center.
* `list_unsubscribe`: The user selected the unsubscribe button in their email client, sent through the `List-Unsubscribe` email header.
* `spam_complaint`: The user classified an email as spam.
* `out_of_band`: A bounce occurred after the recipient MTA accepted an email.

identifiers.description: Contains address information specific to the `channel_id` specified in the event.

address.description: The email address representing the change.

subscription_lists.description: Present when the event represents a change to the user's Subscription List membership rather than a full opt-in or opt-out. When this property is absent, the event applies to the channel as a whole.

canceled.description: An array of Subscription List IDs the user was removed from.

enrolled.description: An array of Subscription List IDs the user was added to.

device.description: Holds information about the email `device` (an individual email channel) the event occurred against.

channel.description: The unique, platform-agnostic channel identifier for a device.

delivery_address.description: The email address of the channel the event occurred against.

device_type.description: Email compliance events use the `EMAIL` device type.

named_user.description: The named user that the channel is associated with; appears only if the channel is associated with a named user.

device.title: Email Device

id.description: Uniquely identifies an event. The data stream will occasionally send duplicate events. Duplicate events will have the same `id`, `type`, `occurred`, `device`, and `body` values but different `offset` and `processed` values. You should use the `id` to deduplicate.

offset.description: An identifier of a location in the stream; used to resume the stream after severing a connection. If you open a stream using the offset value, the stream will resume with the next element in the stream. You should store this value as a string so that you can easily resume the stream if it ends for any reason.

processed.description: When the event was ingested by Airship. There may be some latency between when the event occurred, and when it was processed.

user.description: The current state of the user when the event is emitted, including the contact ID and an optional named user ID.

contact_id.description: The contact identifier for the device.

named_user_id.description: The named user identifier for the device.

user.title: User

title: Subscription event
x-note: These events appear in the stream whenever a user changes their `opted_in` or `opted_out` dates, which may not be meaningful in all cases; a user may opt into an email list on which they're already a member.
